docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Clite

A zero-dependency package for building CLIs. Based on type hints.

The name is inspired by the SQLite

Documentation: https://axemanofic.github.io/clite

Source Code: https://github.com/axemanofic/clite

Warning

This package is currently under development.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pipuvpoetry 
pip install clite

uv add clite

poetry add clite

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from clite import Clite

app = Clite(
    name="myapp",
    description="A small package for creating command line interfaces",
)


@app.command()
def hello(name: str = "world"):
    print(f"Hello, {name}!")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app()

docs.title: Run it
docs.text:
python main.py hello Alice

Output:

Hello, Alice!

docs.title: License
docs.text: This project is licensed under the terms of the MIT license.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in clite! We welcome new contributors and are always open to collaboration.

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Fork this repository.
  2. Create a branch for your changes
  3. Set up the environment — install dependencies using uv: 
    uv sync --all-groups
  4. Make your changes.
  5. Check your code: 
    task all-checks
  6. Commit your changes with a clear description: 
    git commit -m "feat: add new command for ..."
  7. Submit the changes to your fork and create a pull request to the main repository. Please follow the Conventional Commits specification when setting the PR title.

docs.title: Validation Tools
docs.text:
Before creating a PR, please check your code and fix any formatting issues:

  • Linting: 
    uv run ruff check .
uv run mypy .
# or
task lint
  • Formatting: 
    uv run black .
uv run isort .
uv run ruff format .
# or
task format

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Use black for code formatting.
  • Use ruff for linting(check ruff.toml).

docs.title: Reporting Issues
docs.text: If you find a bug or have a suggestion for improvement, please create an issue in this repository. Describe the problem in as much detail as possible and provide a code example if applicable.

docs.title: LIcense
docs.text: All contributions are accepted under the MIT License.

docs.title: Clite
docs.text:
Clite(
    name: str | None = None,
    *,
    description: str | None = None,
    helper: clite.helper.Helper | None = None
)

Clite class.

Class containing all the commands

docs.title: name
docs.text:
name = (
    pathlib.Path(sys.argv[0]).name
    if clite.main.Clite(name) is None
    else clite.main.Clite(name).lower()
)

docs.title: description
docs.text: description = clite.main.Clite(description)

docs.title: 0.2.x
docs.text:
Create a library for creating command line interfaces

  • Make it possible to create a CLI application
  • Make it possible to create a command via a decorator
  • Make it possible to create command arguments
  • Make it possible to create optional command arguments

docs.title: 0.3.x
docs.text:
Create api for creating subcommands

  • Make it possible to create subcommands

docs.title: 0.4.x
docs.text:
Echo message in the console

  • Make it possible to echo a message in the console
